english: I. also sdig-pa rwá-càn (sdig pa rwa can), col. *rá-tse* (*ra tse*), scorpion, also as sign of the zodiac; sdig-pa dkár-po (sdig pa dkar po), nág-po (nag po); sdig-rwá (sdig rwa), the sting of a scorpion; sdig-dúg (sdig dug) the poison of a scorpion; sdig-tsáṅ (sdig tshang) a scorpion's nest; sdig-srín (sdig srin) crab, crawfish, used both as food and medicine Med., but not as designation for the respective sign of the zodiac, v. sub kyím (khyim); sdig-srín-bu (sdig srin bu) Lt. id.? II. (पाप) sin, moral evil as a power, sdig-pa-la yíd-c'es-pa (sdig pa la yid ches pa) Dzl. 222, 11 to believe in sin as such; ˚joms-pa ('joms pa) to conquer sin, as something hostile to man Dom., and so meton. = sinners, adversaries; sometimes perh. for sinfulness, sinful state, but gen. in a concrete sense: offence, trespass, in thought, word, or deed, k'a-na-ma-tó-bai sdig-pa (kha na ma tho ba'i sdig pa), or nyés-pa (nyes pa) prob. a grievous sin Dzl.; also with a genit., rgyál-poi sdig-pa sbyóṅ-ba (rgyal po'i sdig pa sbyong ba) to wash away, to expiate, the king's sin; also ˚dág-pa ('dag pa), sél-ba (sel ba), W. *c'ád-ce* (*chad che*); ˚byáṅ-ba ('byang ba) id., but more in an intransitive or passive sense; so also ˚c'égs-pa ('chegs pa) (gšág-pa (gshag pa), bšags-pa (bshags pa)) to confess, as acc. to Buddhist views, confession is almost tantamount to expiation of sin, cf. also ˚gyód-pa ('gyod pa) and bzód-pa (bzod pa); there seems to be, however, no word strictly corresponding to our 'forgiving' of sin; sdig-(pai)-las (sdig (pa'i) las) a sinful deed; sdig-pa-la dgá-ba (sdig pa la dga' ba) to love sin, to be wicked; sdig-(pai) grogs (sdig (pa'i) grogs) a companion in vice, an associate in crime Dzl.; sdig-pa byéd-pa (sdig pa byed pa), spyód-pa (spyod pa), to commit sin, to sin; sdig-pa mi byéd-pai yul (sdig pa mi byed pa'i yul) a country where no sins are committed, a pious country; sdig-byéd (sdig byed), sdig-spyód (sdig spyod) impious, wicked; a wicked person, sdig-pa-rnams byás-pa (sdig pa rnams byas pa) id. (more accurately: πολλὰ ἡμαρτηκώς) Stg.; sdig-càn (sdig can) id. (sdig-pa-càn (sdig pa can) seems not to be in use); sdig-sgrib (sdig sgrib) the filth, the contamination of sin, sdig-sgrib tams-cád sél-ba (sdig sgrib thams cad sel ba) to cleanse from every defilement of sin Glr. (which the Ommanipadmehūm is sufficient to do); sdig-po (sdig po) a sinner, a bad character, sdig-po c'e (sdig po che) a vile sinner Glr., Mil.; rdig-to-càn (rdig to can), पापीय, = sdig-càn (sdig can), but only as epithet of Dud; sdig-blón (sdig blon) a wicked officer Glr.

pf. bsdigs (bsdigs), fut. bsdig (bsdig), imp. sdigs (sdigs), and sdí-ba (sdi ba), pf. bsdis (bsdis), ft. bsdi (bsdi), 1. to show, to point out, sdigs-mdzúb (sdigs mdzub) a pointing finger, ...la sdigs-mdzúb gtád-pa (...la sdigs mdzub gtad pa) to point at... (with scorn or derision); sdigs-mdzúb nám-mk'a-la gtád (sdigs mdzub nam mkha' la gtad) pointing with the fingers toward heaven, yet not in a 'menacing' (Cs.) way. - 2. to aim C., bsdí(g)s-sa (bsdi(g)s sa) the place that is aimed at, aim, butt; goal Thgy.; bsdís-pai pyógs-su (bsdis pa'i phyogs su) in the direction of the aim Thgy. - 3. to menace, to threaten, cád-pas (chad pas) with punishment Mil. (ni f.); *díg-ce p'i-la* (*dig che phi la*) Ld. as an alarm-shot; ˚dí-la bdág-gis jìgs-pa zìg-gis ma bsdígs-na ('di la bdag gis 'jigs pa zhig gis ma bsdigs na) if I do not threaten him with something frightful, if I do not strike him with fear, Dzl.; sdigs-mo byéd-pa (sdigs mo byed pa) to assume a menacing attitude Mil., to threaten tauntingly Thgy.

a cavity or depression, spáṅ-sdiṅs (spang sdings) a depression on a grassy plain, rí-sdiṅs (ri sdings) on a mountain-ridge; the significations given by Cs., 'middle part, heart, core', were not known to our men of Tashi-lunpo.
1. Sch. = ldib-pa (ldib pa). - 2. Tar. 8, 18 = rtib-pa (rtib pa).

I. adj. pretty, nice, ltá-na sdúg-pa (lta na sdug pa) to look at Dzl.; *tsa-dhi-dúg-pa* (*tsa dhi dug pa*) C. mint, Mentha, ἡδύοσμον; gen. with reference to a person: what is agreeable, pleasing, dear, to a person Ssk.: प्रिय, bdág-gi bu náṅ-gi sdúg-pa-la (bdag gi bu nang gi sdug pa la) the most beloved of my sons Dzl.; ṅai bu sdug (nga'i bu sdug) my dear son Pth., sdúg-par ˚dzín-pa (sdug par 'dzin pa) Dzl., sém-pa (sem pa) Dzl. frq., rtsí-ba (rtsi ba) Mil., to love, c. dat., gen. with regard to parental love; sdúg-par gyúr-ba (sdug par gyur ba) to become dear to a person, to be endeared to, Dzl.; mi-sdúg-pa (mi sdug pa) not fair, ugly, disagreeable, of the body, of a country etc.; mi-sdúg-pai tiṅ-ṅe-˚dzín (mi sdug pa'i ting nge 'dzin) Tar. 10, 11 contemplating one's self and the world as a foul, putrid carcass (v. Tar. Transl. 285, foot of the page); mi-sdúg-par byéd-pa (mi sdug par byed pa) to disfigure, pollute, profane, a temple Dzl.; sdúg-gu (sdug gu) beautiful, pretty, handsome, bud-méd sdúg-gu tams-cád (bud med sdug gu thams cad) all pretty women Dzl.; there is also a form for the fem. gender: sdúg- (sdug-)
